name: Fidelity Bank
description_short: Fidelity Bank is a regional FDIC-insured bank serving eastern Arkansas with five locations and over $500M in assets, offering personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Fidelity Bank is a regional financial institution headquartered in eastern Arkansas with a stated mission to serve the banking needs of neighborhoods and communities in the region. The bank operates five physical locations and manages over $500 million in assets. According to their president and CEO Kirby Carlson, the institution has grown stronger and invites customers to join the "Fidelity Family."

Fidelity Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and commercial banking services. Their personal banking products include savings accounts, personal lending options, credit cards, mobile banking, online banking with bill pay capabilities, e-deposit functionality, and lock box rental services for valuables storage. For business customers, they provide specialized business loan programs designed to meet specific organizational needs. The bank also offers debit cards (MasterCard and VISA branded) with SecurLOCK Equip mobile app technology for card controls.

The bank distinguishes itself through localized community focus and customer service emphasis. Their website features a customer testimonial highlighting service "with dignity & knowledge," and they maintain a 4.7/5 average rating across 140 reviews on Google and Facebook. They offer multiple customer service channels including a main office line (870-735-8700), dedicated debit/credit card support lines, online banking customer service (855-527-5493), and 24/7 AI chatbot assistance through "Champ." Their e-deposit feature allows remote check deposits, and they actively seek customer feedback through reviews.

As a regional bank, Fidelity Bank serves a geographically limited market concentrated in eastern Arkansas. Their online banking and enrollment systems appear to have technical issues based on non-functional endpoints in the provided data. The institution competes with larger national and digital banks, and their five-location footprint limits physical accessibility for customers outside their service area. Their website content suggests solid community banking fundamentals, but limited information is provided regarding specific loan terms, rates, or product details. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: SecurLOCK Equip mobile card control app
Customer service via phone and live chat
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Multiple customer service channels including 24/7 AI chatbot (Champ) and live phone support
Mobile banking and online banking with bill pay and e-deposit functionality
SecurLOCK Equip app for mobile card control and fraud prevention
Strong customer satisfaction with 4.7/5 average rating across 140 reviews
Regional community focus with stated mission to understand local banking needs
Over $500 million in assets with five physical locations
Lock box rental services for secure valuables storage
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to eastern Arkansas (five locations only)
Online banking enrollment system appears to have technical issues based on non-functional endpoints
Website lacks specific information about loan terms, APRs, interest rates, and fee structures
No apparent online account opening capability; limited digital-first banking features compared to neobanks
Limited transparency regarding credit card rewards, benefits, or specific product details

contact.address: 330 W Broadway St, West Memphis, AR 72301

best_for: Eastern Arkansas residents seeking personalized community banking with local decision-making
Small business owners needing customized lending solutions and relationship-based banking
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and traditional brick-and-mortar banking with online options
Community-minded depositors wanting to support local economic development
diagnosis: Fidelity Bank is best suited for eastern Arkansas residents and small business owners who value community banking relationships, personalized service, and FDIC-insured deposits over digital convenience. The primary caveat is their strictly regional presence and limited online banking infrastructure—customers outside their five-location service area or those preferring digital-first banking should explore alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
address: 330 W Broadway St, West Memphis, AR 72301
